Outcome: "successful"
Outcome quote: This result
is consistent with the hypothesis that participants’
choices are positively correlated with their perception
of the percentage of other people who would make the
same choice. || This result is con-
sistent with the hypothesis that participants’ choices are

positively correlated with their perception of the percent-
age of other people who would make the same choice.
Quote source: "p. 460"

Replication article reference: Boyce, V., Mathur, M., & Frank, M. C. (2023). Eleven years of student replication projects provide evidence on the correlates of replicability in psychology. Royal Society Open Science, 10(11). https://doi.org/10.1098/rsos.231240 Outcome: "failed" Outcome quote: sub_rep 0 in dataset Quote source: "https://github.com/forrtproject/FReD-data/issues/19#issuecomment-331901010" Replication article reference: Huang, J. L. (2014). Does cleanliness influence moral judgments? Response effort moderates the effect of cleanliness priming on moral judgments. Frontiers in Psychology, 5. https://doi.org/10.3389/fpsyg.2014.01276 Outcome: "successful" Outcome quote: An online replication (Study 1, N = 214) provided evidence that, when participants exerted low (yet still acceptable) levels of response effort to the experimental material, cleanliness priming led to more lenient moral judgments than neutral priming. Replication article reference: Lee, F. (2013). Replication of “With a clean conscience: Cleanliness reduces the severity of moral judgments” by Schnall, Benton, and Harvey (2008, Psychological Science) [Unpublished manuscript]. Outcome: "failed" Outcome quote: The confirmatory analyses of the current study failed to replicate the findings from the original article. Quote source: "p. 7" Replication article reference: Chaves, J., Fat, J. K., Leukel, P. J., Jordan, A., Koenig, B., & Sharp, M. (2018). Influence of Cleanliness on Moral Judgements: A Direct Replication of Schnall, Benton, and Harvey (2008). OSF Registries. https://doi.org/10.17605/OSF.IO/GEA2S Outcome: "uninformative" Outcome quote: Inconclusive because our results are neither significantly different from the null nor the original. Quote source: "registration: https://osf.io/gea2s" Replication article reference: Besman, M., Dubensky, C., Dunsmore, L., & Daubman, K. (2013). Cleanliness primes less severe moral judgments. Retrieved from: https://web.archive.org/web/20191117063540/http://www.psychfiledrawer.org/replication.php?attempt=MTQ5 Outcome: "successful" Outcome quote: This result is consistent with the results of the original study. Quote source: "Description of methods/results in template" Replication article reference: Arbesfeld, J., Collins, T., Baldwin, D., & Daubman, K. (2014). Clean thoughts lead to less severe moral judgment [Replication of Schnall, Benton, & Harvey, 2008]. PsychFileDrawer.org. Retrieved from https://web.archive.org/web/20191201181415/http://psychfiledrawer.org/replication.php?attempt=MTc3 Outcome: "successful" Outcome quote: Result Type Successful Replication Quote source: "abstract" Replication article reference: Johnson, D. J., Cheung, F., & Donnellan, M. B. (2014). Does Cleanliness Influence Moral Judgments? Social Psychology, 45(3), 209–215. https://doi.org/10.1027/1864-9335/a000186 Outcome: "failed" Outcome quote: We did not find evidence that physical cleanliness reduced the severity of moral judgments using samples sizes that provided over .99 power to detect the original effect sizes.
